calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-23-2018 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-23-2018 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-02-2018 Continued To Oct-23-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Krueger for and with Tanganyika Gordon.
Omar Krashner appeared by telephone for and with Julie Gordon (present).

Mr. Krueger stated that both parties have agreed that this is not an estate for probate and that they can resolve the matter by way of declarations. Mr. Krashner stated that they need to confirm one more asset and that it is not attached to the estate. Both Counsel request a 30-day continuance to confirm that this all happens. Both Petitions for Letters of Administration are continued to October 23, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the anticipation that the Parties will be dismissing these competing petitions.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-02-2018 Continued To Oct-23-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-04-2018 Continued To Oct-02-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Omar Krashna (via Court Call) for (petitioner) Julie Gordon. Mallory Homewood for and with (petitioner) Tanganyika Knight Gordon.

The Court stated that there are competing petitions, one is from the surviving spouse who has authority, the other is from the mother who has lesser authority, that the spouse is now represented and asked if there is a reason she should not be appointed. Mr. Krashna responded that they still have standing objections, that they have lack of faith and fidelity in Ms. Knight-Gordon, that she has advanced items to herself, that there are $600,000.00 in pension and stock options that are not stated in the petition and that they would like a third party fiduciary appointed. Ms. Homewood stated that they tried to file objections but that they were rejected, that Ms. Knight Gordon has acted responsibly, that she notified the Life Insurance company which will benefit the sons, that the pension does not need to go through Probate, and that they request she be appointed as Administrator. Mr. Krashna stated that the pension not going through Probate is okay but that the stock option plan of $400,000.00 does require probate, that the grandchildren maintain their objections, that they have no confidence in Ms. Knight Gordon and that she has sued his client for a motorcycle belonging to the decedent. The Court stated that it is willing to appoint Ms. Gordon but that they need to talk about either Limited Authority or a bond that covers the estate. Mr. Krashna requested a bond in the amount of $600,000.00 until they can confirm that the plans can pass without going through probate. The Court stated that it will continue the matter to address questions of appropriate bond or an alternative to bond, that with respect to the issue of a Guardian ad Litem (GAL) for the minors it is not sure that one is necessary, that it is not sure it is appropriate for Mr. Krashna to represent both the grandmother and the grandchildren as a GAL, that the parties need to talk to each other and that any filings in respect to these issues must be filed no later than September 26, 2018. The Petitions (2) for Letters of Administration were continued to October 2, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Wu. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-04-2018 Continued To Oct-02-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-07-2018 Continued To Sep-04-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-07-2018 Continued To Sep-04-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Omar Krashna appeared by telephone for and with Julie Gordon (present).
Tanganyika Gordon in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that there are competing petitions between the Mother of the Decedent and the Surviving Spouse (Tanganyika Knight Gordon), that there is an objection to the Surviving Spouse's petition and that Tanganyika is now self represented. Ms. Knight Gordon stated that she is planning on getting a new Attorney, but she currently does not have one. The Court stated that while the Surviving Spouse has priority, absent having an Attorney it will be problematic, informed Ms. Knight Gordon that there are going to be very complicated issues that will need to be addressed and urged her to hire an Attorney. Mr. Krashna stated that the value of the real property is still in play, that they haven't determined if the real property will be incorporated into the estate and whether the corporate funds without Beneficiaries will be incorporated into the estate, that he has spoken with Ms. Knight and that everything that was pertinent to their petition has been cleared up. The Court expressed concern stating that Ms. Knight has run through a few Attorneys, that this is not simple, and that there are issues that need to be resolved with regard to what is in the estate and what isn't. The Petitions for Letters of Administration are continued to September 4, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that Ms. Knight Gordon has an Attorney that will file an appearance and that the Parties talk to each other.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-05-2018 Continued To Aug-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-05-2018 Continued To Aug-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-03-2018 Continued To Jul-31-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Deborah Fox for and with Tanganyika Gordon (petitioner)
Omar Krashna for and with Judy Gordon (Petitioner)

Counsel stated that there may not be a need for appointment of administrator in the matter as the assets may be under the threshold amount. Mr. Krashna requested a 60 day continuance to confirm the assets amount, and that the petition for Guardian Ad Litem may also not be necessary. Ms. Fox stated that it would be difficult for her client to be involved in the case for 60 days, and request the shortest possible continuance - 2 weeks - and that competing petitioner had months to investigate assets since the death of the decedent. Mr. Krashna stated that there is a strong likelihood the matter will go off calendar. Counsel will meet and confer. Both petitions for Letters of Administration are continued to July 31, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
